steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses 'as' to explain why Siemowit's existence is debated: our knowledge comes from dubious oral traditions written centuries later. The blank must express how our knowledge relates to those unreliable sources — specifically, that it originates from them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'independent of': states that our knowledge does not come from these sources, which contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'consistent with': says our knowledge agrees with the traditions but does not say it originates from them; the origin is the key relationship. ✗
  • Choice C — 'derived from': directly states that our knowledge originates in these dubious traditions, which explains why the existence is debated. ✓
  • Choice D — 'contrary to': says our knowledge contradicts the traditions, which makes no sense if those traditions are the only source. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
